content.title: The 2 2 Rule
content.body: You cannot have 2 black runs without at least one X separating them. The second row (clue 2 2) is shown incorrectly below - four consecutive black cells. Fix it by placing an X between the two runs. Complete row 2 correctly.

content.title: Clue 3: Overlap Inference
content.body: Each number indicates how many black cells in a run. For clue 3 in a 5-cell row: if the run starts at the beginning, cells 0-2 are black; if it ends at the end, cells 2-4 are black. In both cases the third square is black - so you can mark it. Complete row 3.

content.title: Finish the Puzzle
content.body: Now complete the rest of the puzzle using the row and column clues.
